subject: Telemetry semantics for diagnostic questions, logs, metrics, traces, spans, attributes, correlation IDs, SLOs, alert signals, cardinality, privacy, and sampling
grounding_note: Truth sources cite the whole SKILL.md file to keep the initial eval surface stable while routing boundaries are tightened.

evals.prompt: A background ingestion pipeline needs logs, metrics, spans, correlation IDs, and alert signals designed before implementation. Which skill owns the telemetry model?
The team has diagnostic questions but no event attributes or SLO signals. What should observability-modeling turn those questions into?
The task is to configure Sentry error boundaries and sanitized exception capture only. Should observability-modeling accept?
There is a live production incident and the user wants root-cause debugging. Which boundary should observability-modeling respect?
